pattern: DP where the state encodes the current position in nums, the current segment index in andValues, and the running AND of the current segment; use memoization and observe that the AND can only stay or decrease.
hints: Define dp(i, j, cur_and) = minimum sum to partition nums[i:] into m-j more segments where the current running AND so far is cur_and; transition by extending the current segment or closing it.
The running AND is monotonically non-increasing as you extend a segment; you can close a segment at position i only if cur_and == andValues[j], contributing nums[i] to the cost.
The number of distinct running AND values at any position is O(log(max_val)), so the state space is manageable with memoization.

edge_inputs.description: single segment with exact AND match
AND can reach target only at specific split point
m equals n — each element is its own segment

stress.description: small nums and andValues for feasible brute force
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    # small values to make the problem solvable
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 15) for _ in range(size)]
    m = rng.randint(1, min(size, 3))
    # compute a valid andValues by picking a partition
    # do a random partition and compute ANDs
    import random
    cuts = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, size), m - 1)) if m > 1 else []
    cuts = [0] + cuts + [size]
    andValues = []
    for k in range(m):
        seg = nums[cuts[k]:cuts[k+1]]
        a = seg[0]
        for v in seg[1:]: a &= v
        andValues.append(a)
    return [nums, andValues]
